subject: Physics
title: Carbon Dating
description: A quick win: understand Carbon Dating.

hook.text: Every living thing absorbs Carbon-14. When it dies, the radioactive clock starts ticking.

buildup.text: Living organisms constantly refresh their Carbon-14 by eating and breathing. Dead ones don't.

discovery.text: Willard Libby realized that measuring remaining C-14 reveals how long ago an organism died.

twist.text: Carbon dating only works to about 50,000 years. Older than that, too little C-14 remains to measure.

climax.text: Libby won the 1960 Nobel Prize. His method dated the Dead Sea Scrolls, Stonehenge, and ancient mummies.

punchline.text: A radioactive clock in every bone. The dead tell their own age.

quiz.question: Why does carbon dating stop working after about 50,000 years?
options: Too little Carbon-14 remains to measure accurately
Carbon-14 turns into a different element
Living organisms don't absorb Carbon-14 after that age
